newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Shares fell 3.3% on June 8 with no identifiable fundamental driver (flow/rotation), partially recovered +0.77% today to $207.19. The AI-narrative items in circulation - record $5.1B orders, +$200M raise to FY26 AI revenue outlook, capex lift to ~6% of sales, Phoenix Contact EV charging-inlet acquisition (announced Feb 28, 2026), OFC 2026 CPO/1.6T LRO demos, PCIe Gen 7 and 56G MezzaWave launches - all predate June 6. Standing FQ3 guidance (~$5B sales, ~$2.44 GAAP EPS) unchanged; next earnings late July.

sources.label: Fintool - TE posts record $5.1B orders on AI data center demand
TE Connectivity - OFC 2026 end-to-end optical / CPO demos
TE Connectivity - Phoenix Contact EV charging inlet acquisition (Feb 28, 2026)
Simply Wall St - bull case on AI and EV charging expansion

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved TEL up on the sell-off-with-thesis-intact logic; the June 8 dip (-3.3%, no fundamental driver) plus today's modest +0.77% bounce adds no genuinely new information, so hold with slight positive drift for improved entry asymmetry at -11% YTD.
verdicts.bull: Record $5.1B orders and a raised FY26 AI revenue outlook against a -11% YTD reset means the AI connector/optics franchise is getting cheaper while the backlog grows; flow-driven dips improve the risk-reward.
verdicts.neutral: TEL is a diversified large-cap with transport/industrial ballast; AI is a growing but still minority slice, so it behaves more like a benchmark name than a hidden bottleneck at rank 77.
verdicts.bear: At $60B market cap with heavy sell-side coverage, the AI angle is well understood; auto/industrial end-market softness can swamp the AI growth and keep the YTD drawdown grinding lower.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Late-July FQ3 earnings (~$5B sales, ~$2.44 EPS guided) with another AI revenue outlook raise or order acceleration.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto/industrial demand deterioration or tariff/FX pressure offsetting AI growth, extending the YTD de-rating.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new design wins, guidance, or capacity news since June 6 to alter its optical/high-speed networking centrality; yesterday's move up already captured the scale-out interconnect evidence, and today's +0.77% drift changes nothing.
verdicts.bull: CPO/1.6T LRO architecture work (RAM Photonics FAUs), PCIe Gen 7 and high-density backplane portfolio plus capex lift to ~6% of sales position TEL squarely in the scale-out bandwidth buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Centrality in 800G/1.6T interconnects is real but shared with Amphenol and optics specialists; rank 13 already reflects the networking exposure fairly given the diversified revenue mix.
verdicts.bear: Interconnect content is competitive and pricing-sensitive; if hyperscaler architectures shift faster toward integrated CPO from silicon vendors, discrete connector content per rack could disappoint.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FQ3 print and any new hyperscaler/AI fabric design-win disclosures around 1.6T and CPO programs.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Share loss to Amphenol or architecture shifts (CPO integration) reducing discrete high-speed connector content.
